# Pull Request

## What this PR does

<!-- One or two sentences. -->

## Why

<!-- The motivation. If this fixes an issue, link it: "Fixes #123". -->

## How (brief technical summary)

<!-- High-level. Not a line-by-line code review of your own diff. -->

## Type of change

- [ ] Bug fix
- [ ] New feature
- [ ] Refactor (no behavior change)
- [ ] Documentation
- [ ] Test / CI / tooling
- [ ] Breaking change
- [ ] ADR (architectural decision)

## Checklist

- [ ] Commit messages follow [Conventional Commits](https://www.conventionalcommits.org/)
- [ ] `make` succeeds with no warnings
- [ ] `make format-check` passes
- [ ] `make lint` is clean
- [ ] `make test` passes
- [ ] If this adds a public API, `docs/api/*.md` is updated
- [ ] If this changes behavior, `CHANGELOG.md` has an entry under `Unreleased`
- [ ] If this locks down a non-obvious decision, an ADR is added under `docs/design/adr/`
- [ ] The PR description explains the *why*, not just the *what*

## Screenshots / boot log

<!-- If your change is user-visible (shell output, panic banner, etc.), paste the relevant serial log or screenshot. -->

## Anything reviewers should pay extra attention to?

<!-- Risky areas, parts you're uncertain about, alternative designs you considered. -->
